title: manilkara triflora
intro: Manilkara triflora is a plant species native to tropical regions, also known as the 'Gamboge tree', belonging to the Sapotaceae family. The plant's bark, sap, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Manilkara triflora include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antiparasitic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant's parts are often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, and ointments, highlighting its significance in traditional medicine.
